subject: Math
skill: Tens and Ones as Base-Ten Units

lesson.story_mission: Pack fireflies into lantern crates so every 10 ones becomes 1 ten.
lesson.mini_lesson: A ten is a bundle of 10 ones. Two-digit numbers tell how many tens and how many ones are in the number. For 47, the 4 means 4 tens and the 7 means 7 ones.

worked_examples.text: 10 ones = 1 ten, so 10 loose cubes can be bundled into one ten-rod.

hint_ladder: Circle each full group of 10 ones first.
Read the tens digit to count tens, then read the ones digit to count ones.
Check by adding tens value plus ones value.

guided_practice.prompt: Lantern crates show 10 ones bundled together. 10 ones = how many ten?
Use the place-value chart: 34 has how many tens and how many ones?
Which label matches 5 ten-rods and 7 ones?
Write the number made by 6 tens and 2 ones.
A model has 4 tens and 9 ones. Label both parts.
Which shows that 10 ones can be regrouped?

adaptive_question_bank.prompt: Label both parts of 28: how many tens and how many ones?
Base-ten blocks show 7 tens and 3 ones. What number is shown?
Label both parts of 91: how many tens and how many ones?
A block model has 8 tens and 0 ones. What number is it?
Label both parts of 65: how many tens and how many ones?
A learner has 12 ones. What should they bundle first?
Write the number: 11 tens and 4 ones.
Label both parts of 104: how many tens and how many ones?
Which equation matches 7 tens and 6 ones?
Label both parts of a 39 block model: how many tens and how many ones?
Label both parts of 120: how many tens and how many ones?

checkpoint.prompt: Checkpoint: Label both parts of 58 with tens and ones.
Checkpoint: A model has 9 tens and 6 ones. What number is shown?
Checkpoint: Which statement is true about 10 ones?
Checkpoint: Label both parts of 117 with tens and ones.

misconception_bank.ones_tens_swap: Learner may reverse the tens and ones positions instead of reading tens first.
misconception_bank.ten_not_unitized: Learner may not yet see 10 ones as one composite ten-unit.
misconception_bank.digit_name_value_confusion: Learner may name a digit without connecting it to its place value.

review_bank.prompt: Build 10 ones, trade them for 1 ten, and say the trade aloud.
Read two-digit numbers as tens first, ones second.
Match base-ten blocks, numerals, and place-value charts for the same number.

explanation_fields.why_it_matters: Tens-and-ones unitizing prepares learners to compare, add, and subtract two-digit numbers using place value.

feedback_fields.correct: Great! You named the tens and ones.
feedback_fields.incorrect: Count full groups of ten first, then count loose ones.

level_banks.label: Level 1: Count tens and ones
level_banks.focus: Count tens rods and identify how many tens are shown

questions.prompt: Count the tens rods. 10 is how many tens?
hints: Look at the tens rods first.
Then count the loose ones.
Say the number as tens and ones.
questions.explanation: 10 is 1 tens and 0 ones because each ten rod is worth 10.
questions.feedback: Good model work: 10 = 1 tens and 0 ones.
